channel.title: MATE Desktop Environment – Applications
channel.description: Recent content in Applications on MATE Desktop Environment

item.title: Caja
item.description:
Source Code
Bug Tracker

Caja is the official file manager for the MATE desktop environment. It lets you browse and manage files and directories, access network filesystems, and launch applications. Caja also manages the desktop — drawing the background and providing icons for drives, the trash, and desktop shortcuts.

Features

  • Spatial and browser modes — choose between opening each folder in its own window or navigating within a single browser-style window
  • Tabbed browsing — open multiple locations in a single window
  • Customizable sidebar — bookmarks, tree view, notes, and emblems
  • File previews — thumbnail images, text file snippets, and audio previews in the sidebar
  • Network support — connect to FTP, SFTP, SMB/Windows shares, and WebDAV locations
  • Extensions — additional functionality through plugins (e.g. caja-extensions, caja-dropbox)

Keyboard Shortcuts

Action Shortcut
New window Ctrl+N
New tab Ctrl+T
Close tab Ctrl+W
Go up Alt+Up
Go home Alt+Home
Show hidden files Ctrl+H
Open location bar Ctrl+L
Search Ctrl+F
Rename F2
Properties Alt+Enter

item.title: Pluma
item.description:
Source Code
Bug Tracker

Pluma is the official text editor for the MATE desktop. While designed to be simple and easy to use, it is a capable general-purpose editor suitable for programming, configuration editing, and writing. Its plugin system allows advanced features to be added on demand.

Features

  • Syntax highlighting — for dozens of programming and markup languages
  • Multiple documents — open files as tabs in a single window
  • Search and replace — with full regular expression (PCRE) support
  • Auto-indentation — keeps indentation level when pressing Enter
  • Bracket matching — highlights matching parentheses and braces
  • Line numbers — toggle in the gutter
  • Current line highlight — visually marks the active line
  • Text wrapping — configurable soft and hard wrap modes
  • Plugin system — extend with spell check, snippets, file browser, terminal, and more

Keyboard Shortcuts

Action Shortcut
New file Ctrl+N
Open file Ctrl+O
Save Ctrl+S
Save as Ctrl+Shift+S
Find Ctrl+F
Find and replace Ctrl+H
Go to line Ctrl+I
New tab Ctrl+T
Close tab Ctrl+W
Comment/uncomment Ctrl+M / Ctrl+Shift+M

item.title: Atril
item.description:
Source Code
Bug Tracker

Atril is the official document viewer for the MATE desktop. It supports a wide range of document formats and is suitable for both everyday reading and presentations.

Supported Formats

Format Notes
PDF Portable Document Format
PostScript / EPS PS and Encapsulated PostScript
DjVu Scanned document format
DVI TeX output format
XPS XML Paper Specification
TIFF Multi-page TIFF images
CBR / CBZ Comic book archives

Features

  • Continuous and dual-page view — read documents in single, dual, or continuous scroll mode
  • Presentation mode — fullscreen slideshow for PDF presentations
  • Thumbnails sidebar — quickly jump to any page
  • Bookmarks — save positions within a document
  • Text search — find text across the entire document
  • Copy to clipboard — select and copy text from supported documents
  • Hyperlink navigation — clickable links within documents
  • Printing — print to any configured printer

item.title: Eye of MATE
item.description:
Source Code
Bug Tracker

Eye of MATE (eom) is the official image viewer for the MATE desktop. It is designed to be fast and lightweight while handling large images efficiently. It uses the gdk-pixbuf library and can display a wide range of image formats. Basic editing operations such as rotation and flipping are also supported.

Supported Formats

  • JPEG, PNG, GIF, BMP, TIFF, ICO
  • SVG (with librsvg)
  • WebP, AVIF (depending on gdk-pixbuf loaders installed)
  • Many additional formats via plugins

Features

  • Slideshow — automatically advance through images in a folder
  • Full-screen mode — view images without distractions
  • Zoom and pan — smooth zoom from fit-to-window to 1:1 and beyond
  • Rotation and flip — rotate 90°/180°/270° and flip horizontally or vertically
  • EXIF metadata — view camera settings, GPS coordinates, and other metadata embedded in JPEG files
  • Image collection panel — thumbnail strip for navigating images in a folder
  • Plugin system — extend with features such as map view, fullscreen background, and more

item.title: Engrampa
item.description:
Source Code
Bug Tracker

Engrampa is the official archive manager for the MATE desktop. It is a graphical front-end to command-line archiving tools such as tar, zip, and 7z, forked from File Roller. You can create new archives, add and remove files, inspect contents, and extract files — all without touching the terminal.

Supported Formats

Format Create Extract
ZIP ✓ ✓
TAR, TAR.GZ, TAR.BZ2, TAR.XZ ✓ ✓
7Z ✓ ✓
RAR — ✓
ISO — ✓
JAR ✓ ✓
CAB — ✓
AR / DEB — ✓

Features

  • Create and modify archives with drag-and-drop support
  • View archive contents before extracting
  • Open individual files inside an archive without extracting the whole thing
  • Password-protect ZIP and 7Z archives
  • Extract to a chosen folder or directly to the current directory
  • Caja integration — right-click files to compress or extract

item.title: MATE Terminal
item.description:
Source Code
Bug Tracker

MATE Terminal is the official terminal emulator for the MATE desktop. It provides access to a UNIX shell within the graphical environment, with support for multiple sessions, customizable appearance, and configurable key bindings.

Features

  • Multiple tabs and windows — run several shell sessions side by side
  • Profiles — save different configurations (colors, fonts, shell, working directory) as named profiles
  • Color schemes — built-in palettes plus full custom foreground/background color support
  • Transparent background — configurable background transparency
  • Clickable URLs — open links directly from the terminal output
  • Custom key bindings — remap any terminal shortcut
  • Encoding support — switch character encoding per-session
  • Bell control — visual or audible bell, or silence it entirely

Keyboard Shortcuts

Action Shortcut
New tab Ctrl+Shift+T
New window Ctrl+Shift+N
Close tab Ctrl+Shift+W
Next tab Ctrl+Page Down
Previous tab Ctrl+Page Up
Copy Ctrl+Shift+C
Paste Ctrl+Shift+V
Find Ctrl+Shift+F
Zoom in Ctrl++
Zoom out Ctrl+-
Reset zoom Ctrl+0
Full screen F11

item.title: System Monitor
item.description:
Source Code
Bug Tracker

MATE System Monitor gives you a live view of what is happening on your system. You can inspect running processes, monitor hardware resource usage, and check disk utilization — all from a single window.

Tabs

Processes

Lists all running processes with their PID, user, CPU usage, memory usage, and command. You can:

  • Send signals to processes (including SIGKILL to force-quit)
  • View the full memory map of a process
  • Filter by process name
  • Show all processes or only your own

Resources

Displays scrolling graphs for:

  • CPU — per-core or combined usage over time
  • Memory and swap — current usage and history
  • Network — received and sent bytes per second

File Systems

Lists mounted filesystems with device, type, mountpoint, total size, free space, and usage percentage.

System

Shows basic system information: hostname, kernel version, MATE version, installed memory, and CPU model.

item.title: Calculator
item.description:
Source Code
Bug Tracker

MATE Calculator is the official calculator for the MATE desktop. It is designed to be usable entirely with the keyboard or the mouse, with a visual layout similar to a physical hand-held calculator.

Modes

Mode Description
Basic Everyday arithmetic: addition, subtraction, multiplication, division
Advanced Trigonometry, logarithms, factorials, modulo, and more
Financial Compound interest, present/future value, and loan calculations
Programming Bitwise operations, hex/octal/binary input and display

Features

  • Switch between modes at any time without losing your current calculation
  • Full keyboard input — type expressions naturally
  • History of previous calculations
  • Support for brackets and operator precedence
  • Copy result to clipboard with Ctrl+C

item.title: Control Center
item.description:
Source Code
Bug Tracker

The MATE Control Center is the central place to configure your desktop. It provides a unified launcher for all system and personal preference panels, grouped by category.

Open it from System → Control Center in the panel menu.

Categories

Personal

  • About Me — user name, contact info, and profile photo
  • Preferred Applications — default browser, email client, and terminal
  • Startup Applications — programs that launch automatically at login

Look and Feel

  • Appearance — GTK theme, window borders, icons, and fonts
  • Screensaver — idle time, screensaver selection, and lock settings
  • Windows — window behavior, focus mode, and compositing

Hardware

  • Displays — monitor resolution, orientation, and multi-monitor layout
  • Keyboard — layout, repeat rate, and accessibility settings
  • Mouse — pointer speed, button configuration, and touchpad options
  • Sound — volume levels and sound theme
  • Power Management — battery, display sleep, and suspend settings

Administration

  • Time and Date — system clock and timezone
  • Users and Groups — manage local user accounts

item.title: Mozo
item.description:
Source Code
Bug Tracker

Mozo is the official menu editor for the MATE desktop. It provides a graphical interface for customizing the application menu, following the freedesktop.org menu specification. It is a fork of Alacarte.

Open it from System → Preferences → Main Menu or by right-clicking the Applications menu and choosing Edit Menus.

Features

  • Show and hide entries — toggle the visibility of any application without deleting it
  • Add new items — create launchers for applications, links, or scripts
  • Edit existing entries — change name, icon, command, and description
  • Reorder items — drag entries within a menu to rearrange them
  • Create submenus — add new categories and nest them within existing ones
  • Undo and redo — revert accidental changes
  • Changes are stored in the user’s ~/.local/share/applications and ~/.config/menus directories and do not affect system-wide menu files
